endpoints.name: List Routing Numbers
endpoints.description: You can use this API to confirm if a routing number is valid, such as when a user is providing you with bank account details. Since routing numbers uniquely identify a bank, this will always return 0 or 1 entry. In Sandbox, the only valid routing number for this method is 110000000.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate an Account Statement being created
endpoints.description: Simulates an [Account Statement](#account-statements) being created for an account. In production, Account Statements are generated once per month.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Complete a Sandbox Account Transfer
endpoints.description: If your account is configured to require approval for each transfer, this endpoint simulates the approval of an [Account Transfer](#account-transfers). You can also approve sandbox Account Transfers in the dashboard. This transfer must first have a `status` of `pending_approval`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{ACCOUNT_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real account_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Return a Sandbox ACH Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates the return of an [ACH Transfer](#ach-transfers) by the Federal Reserve due to an error condition. This will also create a Transaction to account for the returned funds. This transfer must first have a `status` of `submitted`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{ACH_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real ach_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Submit a Sandbox ACH Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates the submission of an [ACH Transfer](#ach-transfers) to the Federal Reserve. This transfer must first have a `status` of `pending_approval` or `pending_submission`. In production, Increase submits ACH Transfers to the Federal Reserve three times per day on weekdays. Since sandbox ACH Transfers are not submitted to the Federal Reserve, this endpoint allows you to skip that delay and transition the ACH Transfer to a status of `submitted`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{ACH_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real ach_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Simulate an authorization on a Card
endpoints.description: Simulates a purchase authorization on a [Card](#cards). Depending on the balance available to the card and the `amount` submitted, the authorization activity will result in a [Pending Transaction](#pending-transactions) of type `card_authorization` or a [Declined Transaction](#declined-transactions) of type `card_decline`. You can pass either a Card id or a [Digital Wallet Token](#digital-wallet-tokens) id to simulate the two different ways purchases can be made.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulates advancing the state of a card dispute
endpoints.description: After a [Card Dispute](#card-disputes) is created in production, the dispute will be reviewed. Since no review happens in sandbox, this endpoint simulates moving a Card Dispute into a rejected or accepted state. A Card Dispute can only be actioned one time and must have a status of `pending_reviewing`.

body.explanation: This was a valid recurring transaction

endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CARD_DISPUTE_ID}} with a real card_dispute_id.

endpoints.name: Simulate a refund on a card
endpoints.description: Simulates refunding a card transaction. The full value of the original sandbox transaction is refunded.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate settling a card authorization
endpoints.description: Simulates the settlement of an authorization by a card acquirer. After a card authorization is created, the merchant will eventually send a settlement. This simulates that event, which may occur many days after the purchase in production. The amount settled can be different from the amount originally authorized, for example, when adding a tip to a restaurant bill.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Reject a Sandbox Check Deposit
endpoints.description: Simulates the rejection of a [Check Deposit](#check-deposits) by Increase due to factors like poor image quality. This Check Deposit must first have a `status` of `pending`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CHECK_DEPOSIT_ID}} with a real check_deposit_id.

endpoints.name: Return a Sandbox Check Deposit
endpoints.description: Simulates the return of a [Check Deposit](#check-deposits). This Check Deposit must first have a `status` of `submitted`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CHECK_DEPOSIT_ID}} with a real check_deposit_id.

endpoints.name: Submit a Sandbox Check Deposit
endpoints.description: Simulates the submission of a [Check Deposit](#check-deposits) to the Federal Reserve. This Check Deposit must first have a `status` of `pending`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CHECK_DEPOSIT_ID}} with a real check_deposit_id.

endpoints.name: Deposit a Sandbox Check Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates a [Check Transfer](#check-transfers) being deposited at a bank. This transfer must first have a `status` of `mailed`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CHECK_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real check_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Mail a Sandbox Check Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates the mailing of a [Check Transfer](#check-transfers), which happens once per weekday in production but can be sped up in sandbox. This transfer must first have a `status` of `pending_approval` or `pending_submission`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{CHECK_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real check_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Simulate digital wallet provisioning for a card
endpoints.description: Simulates a user attempting add a [Card](#cards) to a digital wallet such as Apple Pay.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate a tax document being created
endpoints.description: Simulates an tax document being created for an account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate an ACH Transfer to your account
endpoints.description: Simulates an inbound ACH transfer to your account. This imitates initiating a transaction to an Increase account from a different financial institution. The transfer may be either a credit or a debit depending on if the `amount` is positive or negative. The result of calling this API will be either a [Transaction](#transactions) or a [Declined Transaction](#declined-transactions) depending on whether or not the transfer is allowed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate a Real Time Payments Transfer to your account
endpoints.description: Simulates an inbound Real Time Payments transfer to your account. Real Time Payments are a beta feature.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate an Inbound Wire Drawdown request being created
endpoints.description: Simulates the receival of an [Inbound Wire Drawdown Request](#inbound-wire-drawdown-requests).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Simulate a Wire Transfer to your account
endpoints.description: Simulates an inbound Wire Transfer to your account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Reverse a Sandbox Wire Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates the reversal of a [Wire Transfer](#wire-transfers) by the Federal Reserve due to error conditions. This will also create a [Transaction](#transaction) to account for the returned funds. This Wire Transfer must first have a `status` of `complete`.'
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{WIRE_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real wire_transfer_id.

endpoints.name: Submit a Sandbox Wire Transfer
endpoints.description: Simulates the submission of a [Wire Transfer](#wire-transfers) to the Federal Reserve. This transfer must first have a `status` of `pending_approval` or `pending_creating`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{INCREASE_API_KEY}} with your INCREASE API KEY. Replace {{WIRE_TRANSFER_ID}} with a real wire_transfer_id.
